perturbation.original_text: Ross & Moncure recognizes Confidential Information to be any information or personal data that it receives from the Client in the process of tax preparation or planning .
perturbation.changed_text: Ross & Moncure recognizes Confidential Information to be *only certain* information or personal data *explicitly marked* as confidential by the Client in the process of tax preparation or planning.
perturbation.explanation: This change narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information'. Originally, any information received during tax preparation was considered confidential. The modification now states that only information 'explicitly marked' as confidential is protected. This creates a contradiction because it suggests that some client data received might not be treated as confidential unless specifically designated, contradicting the implied broader protection earlier in the section. This change affects the security of the information, creating uncertainty in enforcement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information. “Confidential Information” means non -public information that a party to this Agreement (“Disclosing Party ”) designates as being confidential to the party that receives such information (“Receiving Party”) or which, under the circumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential by the Receiving Party. Ross & Moncure recognizes Confidential Information to be *only certain* information or personal data *explicitly marked* as confidential by the Client in the process of tax preparation or planning .
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Confidential Information' broadly as any information received from the client. The changed text narrows this definition to only information 'explicitly marked' as confidential. The contradicted text contains the changed text and thus this directly conflicts with the broader definition, creating a contradiction and affecting the information security and enforcement certainty.

perturbation.original_text: Under no circumstances may it disclose such data to third parties witho ut written authorization from the Client, even merely for safekeeping.
perturbation.changed_text: Under *most* circumstances may it disclose such data to third parties witho ut written authorization from the Client, even merely for safekeeping.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly prohibits disclosure to third parties without written authorization. By changing 'no circumstances' to 'most circumstances', the clause now implies there are undisclosed situations where disclosure *is* allowed without authorization. This directly contradicts the intention of the original clause and weakens the protection of confidential information. It introduces ambiguity and could lead to disputes over when unauthorized disclosure is permissible. This creates contradiction in section 2, since now is unclear if the information is confidential or not
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations Regarding Confidential Information. Ross & Moncure pledges not to disclose any information to which it has access in the process of the performance of a Client engagement, *except when legally compelled to do so*. Ross & Moncure also agrees to only request of the Client information necessary to carry out services requested by the Client. Ross & Moncure shall process information and data provided in accordance with the instructions given by the Client and shall refr ain from recording, reproducing, or storing data for superfluous reasons. This prohibition affects both hard -copy data and data on any electronic, magn etic, analogue or digital medium . Under *most* circumstances may it disclose such data to third parties witho ut written authorization from the Client, even merely for safekeeping.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'Under no circumstances may it disclose such data to third parties without written authorization'. The changed text modifies this to 'Under *most* circumstances may it disclose such data to third parties without written authorization'. This change introduces a contradiction because it implies that there are now undisclosed circumstances where disclosure *is* allowed without authorization, which directly conflicts with the explicit prohibition in the original clause and creates ambiguity. The 'Obligations Regarding Confidential Information' section now becomes unclear regarding the confidentiality of the data, which makes it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Ross & Moncure recognizes that the duties of secrecy and non -disclosure continue to apply indefinitely even after all service engagements have terminated.
perturbation.changed_text: Ross & Moncure recognizes that the duties of secrecy and non -disclosure continue to apply *for a period of 5 years* even after all service engagements have terminated.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement stipulated that confidentiality obligations last indefinitely. Now, those obligations are limited to five years. This direct contradiction introduces ambiguity regarding the duration of confidentiality. Clients relying on the original indefinite term might be harmed after the five-year period, while Ross & Moncure could face disputes for disclosures made after that period but assumed to be perpetually confidential under the original terms. This change creates contradiction in section 2
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligations Regarding Confidential Information. Ross & Moncure pledges not to disclose any information to which it has access in the process of the performance of a Client engagement, *except when legally compelled to do so*. Ross & Moncure also agrees to only request of the Client information necessary to carry out services requested by the Client. Ross & Moncure shall process information and data provided in accordance with the instructions given by the Client and shall refr ain from recording, reproducing, or storing data for superfluous reasons. This prohibition affects both hard -copy data and data on any electronic, magn etic, analogue or digital medium . Under *most* circumstances may it disclose such data to third parties witho ut written authorization from the Client, even merely for safekeeping. Ross & Moncure recognizes that the duties of secrecy and non -disclosure continue to apply *for a period of 5 years* even after all service engagements have terminated.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that non-disclosure duties applied indefinitely, while the changed text limits this to 5 years. The contradicted text contains both the original indefinite statement AND the modified 5-year limit. This creates a direct and obvious contradiction within the contract, as the same clause now states two different durations for the same obligation, making it impossible to determine the correct duration. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Ross & Moncure shall bear the burden of any damages arising from negligence and/or lack of confidentiality, improper use, processing or communication of personal data, or any other infringeme nt of data protection rules. The full extent of these damages shall be determined by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: Ross & Moncure *may not* bear the burden of any damages arising from negligence and/or lack of confidentiality, improper use, processing or communication of personal data, or any other infringeme nt of data protection rules. The full extent of these damages shall be determined by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause clearly states that Ross & Moncure is responsible for damages resulting from breaches of confidentiality. By inserting the word 'may not,' this responsibility is removed. This creates uncertainty about liability and contradicts the implied protection promised by the rest of the agreement. It is now unclear if Ross & Moncure can be held accountable for breaches, potentially exposing clients to risk. This change creates contradiction in section 3, since is not clear who is responsible for data breach
perturbation.contradicted_text: Rights and Remedies Ross & Moncure shall notify the undersigned Client immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by an employee of Ross & Moncure , and will cooperate with the Disclosing Party in every reasonable way to help the Disclosing Party to regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure. Ross & Moncure *may not* bear the burden of any damages arising from negligence and/or lack of confidentiality, improper use, processing or communication of personal data, or any other infringeme nt of data protection rules. The full extent of these damages shall be determined by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text states Ross & Moncure 'shall bear the burden', while the changed text says they 'may not bear the burden'. This directly contradicts the obligation, creating a clear conflict. The contradicted text includes this contradictory statement, which leads to the interpretation that the 'Rights and Remedies' section is now unclear regarding responsibility for unauthorized use or disclosure, since the changed text introduces ambiguity where there should be certainty. This directly adheres to the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition, where a clause is placed in multiple locations (in this case, the same location) with conflicting implications.
